**[Section 1 --- Introduction and Scope]{.underline}**

The purpose of this document is to present the proposed performance testing plan for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System in support of a future traditional 510(k) premarket notification submission to the Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) of the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA). This testing plan is submitted as a supporting document within Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc.\'s Pre-Submission (Q-Sub) package and is intended to facilitate meaningful FDA feedback on the adequacy and completeness of proposed testing prior to initiation of formal studies.

The VascuClear 3000 is a single-use, over-the-wire catheter-based mechanical thrombectomy system consisting of three principal components:

1\. **VascuClear Catheter** --- A 10 French (Fr) single-use catheter with a 65 cm working length, featuring a rotating helical impeller at the distal tip for mechanical thrombus fragmentation and removal, and an integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen that permits simultaneous local delivery of tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) at the thrombectomy site.

2\. **VascuClear Console** --- A reusable electromechanical drive unit that provides variable-speed impeller rotation at speeds up to 12,000 revolutions per minute (RPM) and integrated aspiration pump capability generating negative pressure up to −650 mmHg for thrombus aspiration.

3\. **Proprietary Thrombolytic Infusion Lumen** --- Integrated into the catheter shaft, this dedicated lumen enables the treating physician to deliver physician-specified thrombolytic agents (e.g., tPA) locally at the site of thrombectomy during the mechanical thrombectomy procedure.

The intended use of the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System is the percutaneous removal of thrombus and simultaneous local delivery of physician-specified thrombolytic agents in adult patients with acute deep vein thrombosis (DVT) of the iliofemoral venous segment, with symptom onset within 14 days or fewer.

From a regulatory standpoint, Clearfield intends to submit the VascuClear 3000 via the traditional 510(k) pathway under product code GXD (\"Catheter, Thrombectomy\"), regulated under 21 CFR 870.1250. The identified predicate device is the ThrombEx 200 Thrombectomy Catheter (K192847), manufactured by Vasogen Therapeutics, Inc. and cleared by CDRH on August 14, 2020.

The testing plan described herein is organized into seven categories, reflecting the full scope of performance and safety characterization required to demonstrate substantial equivalence to the predicate device:

> • Biocompatibility testing (Section 2)
>
> • Mechanical performance testing (Section 3)
>
> • Electrical safety testing (Section 4)
>
> • Software verification and validation (Section 5)
>
> • Sterilization validation (Section 6)
>
> • Simulated-use bench performance testing (Section 7)
>
> • Drug delivery characterization testing (Section 8)

This document should be read in conjunction with the companion documents included in the Pre-Submission package: the device description document (DOC*001), predicate comparison document (DOC*002), clinical synopsis (DOC*003), and draft Instructions for Use (DOC*005).

Testing will be conducted at Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories (biocompatibility and sterilization validation studies), Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc. (electrical safety and software verification and validation), and Clearfield Medical Devices\' internal R&D facility in Minnetonka, Minnesota (mechanical performance, bench performance, and drug delivery characterization). Each testing laboratory\'s qualifications and accreditations are further described in Appendix B.

**[Section 2 --- Biocompatibility Testing]{.underline}**

**[2.1 --- Overview and Rationale]{.underline}**

Biocompatibility testing for the VascuClear Catheter will be conducted in accordance with ISO 10993-1:2018, *Biological evaluation of medical devices --- Part 1: Evaluation and review within a risk management process*. The objective of the biocompatibility testing program is to evaluate the biological safety of the catheter materials that come into direct or indirect contact with the patient\'s blood and vascular tissue during clinical use.

The VascuClear Catheter is classified as a blood-contacting device with limited contact duration (less than 24 hours) per ISO 10993-1:2018, Table A.1. The catheter is inserted percutaneously for the thrombectomy procedure, which is typically completed within a single procedural session. Based on this classification, the biological evaluation endpoints have been selected from the matrix provided in Table A.1 of ISO 10993-1:2018 for devices with blood contact and limited exposure duration.

Given the limited contact duration classification, subchronic implantation testing per ISO 10993-6 is not applicable and has been explicitly excluded from the proposed testing plan. Similarly, subchronic systemic toxicity testing per ISO 10993-11 is not required for devices with limited contact duration and is excluded from the proposed program. Genotoxicity testing per ISO 10993-3 is likewise not required for limited-contact-duration devices and has been excluded. Chronic systemic toxicity testing is also not applicable.

All biocompatibility studies will be performed at Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories, a GLP-compliant contract testing organization accredited under ISO 17025. Testing protocols will follow the applicable parts of the ISO 10993 series as detailed in Section 2.2 below. A biological evaluation report summarizing findings across all endpoints will be prepared for inclusion in the 510(k) submission.

**[2.2 --- Proposed Biocompatibility Endpoints]{.underline}**

The following biocompatibility tests are proposed for the VascuClear Catheter. The selected endpoints are based on the device\'s classification as a blood-contacting device with limited contact duration per ISO 10993-1:2018, Table A.1.

**Table 2-1: Proposed Biocompatibility Tests**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Test Endpoint**         **Applicable Standard**     **Test Method**                                                                                                                                                                       **Testing Laboratory**
  ------------------------- --------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------
  Cytotoxicity              ISO 10993-5                 MEM elution method; L-929 mouse fibroblast cell line; qualitative and quantitative assessment                                                                                         Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories

  Sensitization             ISO 10993-10                Guinea pig maximization test (GPMT) or murine local lymph node assay (LLNA), at the discretion of the testing laboratory                                                              Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories

  Irritation                ISO 10993-23                Intracutaneous reactivity in rabbits; polar and non-polar extract injections; 24-, 48-, and 72-hour observation                                                                       Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories

  Acute Systemic Toxicity   ISO 10993-11                Intravenous injection in mice; polar and non-polar extracts; observation at 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour intervals                                                                   Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories

  Hemocompatibility         ISO 10993-4                 Hemolysis testing (direct and indirect contact methods), complement activation (C3a, SC5b-9 ELISA), and thrombogenicity assessment (partial thromboplastin time, platelet adhesion)   Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories

  Pyrogenicity              ISO 10993-11; USP \<151\>   Rabbit pyrogen test and/or bacterial endotoxin (LAL) assay per USP \<85\>                                                                                                             Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following biocompatibility endpoints have been **excluded** based on the limited contact duration classification:

> • **Subchronic Systemic Toxicity (ISO 10993-11)** --- Not required for limited contact devices per ISO 10993-1:2018, Table A.1. Subchronic systemic toxicity testing is applicable only to devices classified under prolonged or permanent contact durations.
>
> • **Implantation Testing (ISO 10993-6)** --- Not applicable. The VascuClear Catheter is not an implant, and the device contact duration is limited to less than 24 hours. Implantation studies are therefore unnecessary.
>
> • **Genotoxicity (ISO 10993-3)** --- Not required for limited contact duration devices. Genotoxicity endpoints are indicated by Table A.1 only for prolonged and permanent contact devices.
>
> • **Chronic Systemic Toxicity** --- Not applicable for any device with limited or prolonged contact duration. Excluded without further analysis.

In addition to the biological tests listed above, material characterization in accordance with ISO 10993-18 will be performed to support the biological evaluation. This chemical characterization will include identification of extractable and leachable substances from the catheter materials under physiologically relevant conditions, providing analytical data to complement the in vivo and in vitro biological testing.

**Acceptance Criteria:** For each biocompatibility endpoint, the acceptance criterion is that the test article must meet the pass criteria established in the respective ISO 10993 series test standard. No evidence of unacceptable biological response --- as defined by the applicable scoring systems, grading scales, or quantitative thresholds specified in each standard --- shall be observed. Any test result that does not meet the pass criteria will be documented as a non-conformance and subject to investigation and risk assessment before inclusion in the biological evaluation report.

**[2.3 --- Materials Evaluated]{.underline}**

The following materials comprising the VascuClear Catheter will be subject to biocompatibility evaluation, including both the biological tests described in Section 2.2 and the chemical characterization performed under ISO 10993-18:

> • **Catheter Shaft** --- Polyurethane/Pebax (polyether block amide) blend; this material forms the primary body of the catheter shaft and is the largest surface area component in contact with the patient\'s blood and vasculature.
>
> • **Impeller Housing** --- Stainless steel 316L; located at the distal tip of the catheter, the impeller housing encloses the rotating helical impeller and is in direct contact with blood during the thrombectomy procedure.
>
> • **Helical Impeller** --- Nitinol (nickel-titanium alloy); the rotating impeller element is in direct, dynamic contact with blood and thrombus material during device operation.
>
> • **Infusion Lumen Liner** --- Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE); the inner lining of the thrombolytic infusion lumen, in contact with any fluid delivered through the infusion channel.
>
> • **Hydrophilic Coating** --- Applied to the outer surface of the catheter distal tip to reduce friction during insertion and navigation; specific coating chemistry to be characterized under ISO 10993-18.
>
> • **Adhesives and Bonding Agents** --- Used at catheter-to-hub junctions, impeller-to-shaft bonds, and lumen-to-shaft bonds; specific adhesive formulations to be identified and characterized.

The VascuClear Console is a reusable electromechanical drive unit that does not come into direct or indirect contact with the patient. Accordingly, the console is excluded from biocompatibility testing. Biological evaluation is limited to the patient-contacting catheter component and its constituent materials.

Material characterization under ISO 10993-18 will include chemical identification of all materials, quantification of extractable substances using exhaustive extraction methods (polar and non-polar solvents), and analytical profiling using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS), liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS), and inductively coupled plasma spectroscopy (ICP) as appropriate.

**[Section 3 --- Mechanical Performance Testing]{.underline}**

**[3.1 --- Overview]{.underline}**

Mechanical performance testing will characterize the physical and mechanical properties of the VascuClear Catheter to ensure that the device performs safely and effectively in a manner consistent with its intended clinical use. Testing will assess the catheter\'s ability to navigate tortuous vascular anatomy, withstand the mechanical stresses of insertion and operation, and maintain structural integrity under worst-case conditions.

Applicable standards and test methods include internal Clearfield test protocols, supplemented by reference to ASTM and ISO standards where applicable. All mechanical performance testing will be conducted at Clearfield Medical Devices\' internal R&D testing facility in Minnetonka, Minnesota, with certain specialized analyses subcontracted to Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories as needed.

**[3.2 --- Catheter Mechanical Properties]{.underline}**

The following mechanical performance tests are proposed to characterize the VascuClear Catheter:

**Pushability Testing.** The force required to advance the VascuClear Catheter through a simulated tortuous vascular anatomy will be measured using a calibrated force gauge. The test model consists of a silicone vascular anatomy simulator incorporating 90° and 180° bends representative of iliofemoral venous anatomy. The catheter will be advanced through the model over a standard 0.035-inch guidewire at a controlled advancement rate of 10 mm/s. Peak and average push forces will be recorded. The acceptance criterion is that the catheter shall advance through the full length of the simulated anatomy without buckling or kinking at push forces not exceeding 5 N.

**Torqueability Testing.** Torque transmission fidelity will be assessed by applying controlled rotational input at the proximal hub and measuring the resulting rotation at the distal tip. Testing will be performed with the catheter loaded into the silicone vascular model with 90° and 180° bends. The acceptance criterion is a 1:1 torque transmission ratio ±15% (measured as distal tip angular displacement relative to proximal hub angular displacement) through both 90° and 180° bends.

**Kink Resistance.** The catheter will be subjected to a three-point bend test at multiple radii of curvature to determine the minimum bend radius at which kinking occurs. The catheter will be progressively bent over mandrels of decreasing radius from 50 mm down to 10 mm. Fluoroscopic or visual inspection will be used to identify the onset of kinking. The acceptance criterion is a minimum bend radius without kink of 20 mm or less.

**Tensile Strength.** Bond strength at critical junctions will be evaluated via tensile pull testing using a calibrated universal testing machine. Specific joints to be tested include the catheter-to-hub bond and the impeller-to-shaft bond. The catheter or component will be secured in fixtures and subjected to axial tensile load at a crosshead speed of 50 mm/min until failure. The acceptance criterion is a minimum pull force of 10 N per joint before separation or failure occurs.

**Burst Pressure Testing.** The infusion lumen of the VascuClear Catheter will be pressurized with saline using a calibrated pressure source. The infusion lumen will be occluded at the distal tip and pressurized at a controlled rate until either burst occurs or a target pressure of 3× the maximum rated infusion pressure is reached and held for 60 seconds. The acceptance criterion is that the infusion lumen shall withstand a minimum burst pressure of 3× the maximum rated infusion pressure without leakage, rupture, or structural failure.

**Table 3-1: Catheter Mechanical Performance Summary**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Parameter**                          **Test Method**                      **Sample Size**   **Acceptance Criterion**
  -------------------------------------- ------------------------------------ ----------------- ----------------------------------------------
  Pushability                            Force gauge / silicone model         n = 10            ≤ 5 N; no buckling or kinking

  Torqueability                          Torque transmission measurement      n = 10            1:1 ± 15% through 90° and 180° bends

  Kink Resistance                        Three-point bend at multiple radii   n = 10            Minimum bend radius ≤ 20 mm without kink

  Tensile Strength (catheter-to-hub)     Axial pull test, 50 mm/min           n = 10            ≥ 10 N minimum pull force

  Tensile Strength (impeller-to-shaft)   Axial pull test, 50 mm/min           n = 10            ≥ 10 N minimum pull force

  Burst Pressure (infusion lumen)        Hydrostatic pressure test            n = 10            ≥ 3× max rated infusion pressure; no leakage
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[3.3 --- Impeller Performance and Fatigue Testing]{.underline}**

**Impeller Rotational Performance.** The VascuClear impeller will be evaluated across its full operational speed range to verify smooth, unimpeded rotation. The catheter will be submerged in a 37°C saline bath and the console activated at impeller speeds of 2,000 RPM, 4,000 RPM, 6,000 RPM, 8,000 RPM, 10,000 RPM, and 12,000 RPM. At each speed setting, the impeller will be visually inspected (using high-speed camera imaging where applicable) for evidence of binding, wobble, excessive vibration, or irregular rotation. Rotational speed will be independently verified using an optical tachometer. Testing will be performed on a minimum of six catheter units (n = 6). The acceptance criterion is that the impeller shall rotate freely and consistently at all tested speeds without evidence of binding, wobble, or vibration exceeding predefined amplitude thresholds.

**Aspiration Flow Rate Verification.** The aspiration performance of the VascuClear Console will be characterized at three clinically relevant negative pressure settings: −200 mmHg, −400 mmHg, and −650 mmHg. The catheter aspiration lumen will be connected to the console and submerged in a 37°C saline reservoir. At each pressure setting, the volume of saline aspirated over a 60-second period will be measured using a calibrated graduated cylinder. Aspiration flow rate will be calculated in mL/min. Design specification flow rates are 120 mL/min at −200 mmHg, 280 mL/min at −400 mmHg, and 450 mL/min at −650 mmHg. The acceptance criterion is that the measured aspiration flow rate shall be within ±15% of the design specification at each pressure setting. A minimum of six console-catheter combinations will be tested (n = 6).

**Impeller Fatigue Testing.** The impeller shall undergo fatigue testing for a minimum of 500 rotational cycles at maximum rated speed (12,000 RPM) in a 37°C saline bath. One rotational cycle is defined as one complete revolution (360°) of the impeller. The fatigue test is designed to assess the structural integrity and durability of the impeller and its attachment to the catheter shaft under simulated worst-case operational conditions. The impeller will be operated continuously at 12,000 RPM until 500 complete rotational cycles have been recorded, at which point the console will be deactivated and the catheter removed for post-test evaluation.

Post-test inspection will include:

> • Visual inspection of the impeller under 10× magnification for evidence of deformation, fracture, crack initiation, or detachment from the catheter shaft
>
> • Dimensional measurement of the impeller using calibrated calipers and optical comparator to detect dimensional changes relative to pre-test measurements
>
> • Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) imaging of impeller surfaces at 100× and 500× magnification to detect surface damage, fatigue striations, or micro-cracking
>
> • Filtration of the saline bath through a 25 µm filter with microscopic analysis of the filtrate to detect particulate debris

A minimum of six catheter units (n = 6) will be subjected to the impeller fatigue test protocol. The acceptance criteria are as follows: no evidence of impeller fracture, deformation, or detachment from the catheter shaft; no dimensional change exceeding ±0.05 mm in any measured dimension; and no particulate debris greater than 25 µm detected in the filtered saline bath.

**Particulate Generation Testing.** In addition to the fatigue test filtrate analysis, a dedicated particulate generation test will be conducted. The VascuClear Catheter impeller will be operated at maximum speed (12,000 RPM) in a closed-loop circulating saline system at 37°C for a continuous 10-minute period. Following operation, the circulating saline will be collected and analyzed for particulate count per USP \<788\> (Particulate Matter in Injections), using both light obscuration and microscopic methods. Acceptance criteria will be based on the USP \<788\> limits for large-volume parenterals. A minimum of three catheter units (n = 3) will be tested.

**[Section 4 --- Electrical Safety Testing]{.underline}**

**[4.1 --- Overview and Applicable Standards]{.underline}**

Electrical safety testing will be performed on the VascuClear Console to verify compliance with applicable safety requirements for medical electrical equipment intended for use in clinical settings. The VascuClear Console is a reusable, AC-powered electromechanical drive unit that provides impeller rotation and aspiration pump functionality for the VascuClear Catheter.

The applicable standard for electrical safety testing is IEC 60601-1:2012, *Medical electrical equipment --- Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance*, including applicable national deviations for the United States market as recognized by FDA. Testing will be performed by Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc., an OSHA Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory (NRTL) accredited for IEC 60601-1 testing.

The VascuClear Console is classified under IEC 60601-1 as Class I equipment (protective earth connection), with a Type BF applied part. The applied part designation reflects the fact that the VascuClear Catheter, which is connected to the console via the drive coupling and aspiration tubing, makes direct contact with the patient\'s blood during clinical use. The Type BF classification provides an appropriate level of protection against electrical shock for a device with a conductive fluid path to the patient.

All electrical safety testing will be performed on production-equivalent console units. The proposed test program is described in Section 4.2 below.

**[4.2 --- Proposed Electrical Safety Tests]{.underline}**

The following electrical safety tests will be performed on the VascuClear Console in accordance with IEC 60601-1:2012:

1\. **Protective Earth Resistance.** Measurement of resistance between the protective earth terminal of the power cord plug and accessible conductive parts of the console enclosure. Acceptance criterion: ≤ 0.1 Ω per IEC 60601-1 Clause 8.6.

2\. **Leakage Current Measurements.** Measurement of earth leakage current, enclosure leakage current, and patient leakage current under both normal condition and single fault condition (open earth conductor, open neutral conductor). All measurements will be taken at rated supply voltage ±10%. Acceptance criteria: per IEC 60601-1 Table 3 and Table 4 limits for Type BF applied parts.

3\. **Dielectric Strength (Hi-Pot) Testing.** Application of test voltage between mains circuit and accessible parts, and between mains circuit and applied part circuit, to verify adequate insulation. Test voltages per IEC 60601-1 Clause 8.8. Acceptance criterion: no breakdown or flashover; leakage current during test within specified limits.

4\. **Mechanical Strength of Enclosure.** Impact, drop, and steady-state force tests on the console enclosure per IEC 60601-1 Clause 15.3. Acceptance criterion: no compromise of basic safety or essential performance after application of mechanical stress; no exposure of hazardous voltages.

5\. **Thermal Testing.** Measurement of surface temperatures on accessible parts of the console during continuous operation at maximum rated load (impeller at 12,000 RPM, aspiration at −650 mmHg) for a minimum of one hour. Temperature measurement by thermocouple. Acceptance criterion: surface temperatures shall not exceed the limits specified in IEC 60601-1 Table 24 for parts that may be touched during normal use.

6\. **Interrupted Power Supply Behavior.** Evaluation of console behavior upon loss and subsequent restoration of AC mains power during operation. The console will be operated at maximum settings and mains power will be interrupted for periods of 1 second, 5 seconds, and 30 seconds, followed by restoration. The test will verify that the console returns to a safe state (impeller stopped, aspiration off) upon power loss and does not automatically resume operation upon power restoration without user action. Acceptance criterion: console enters safe stop state upon power loss; no unintended impeller rotation or aspiration upon power restoration.

7\. **Marking and Labeling Compliance.** Verification that the console exterior markings, labels, and symbols comply with IEC 60601-1 Clause 7, including safety signs, ratings, fuse markings, and applied part identification. Acceptance criterion: all required markings present, legible, and durable per the clause requirements.

All electrical safety testing will be conducted on a minimum sample size of three (n = 3) production-equivalent VascuClear Console units. Each unit will undergo the complete battery of tests described above. Test reports will be prepared by Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc. and included in the 510(k) submission.

**[Section 5 --- Software Verification and Validation]{.underline}**

**[5.1 --- Software Description]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Console incorporates embedded firmware (software) that is integral to the device\'s operation and safety. The console software provides the following functions:

> • **Impeller Speed Control** --- The software manages variable-speed motor drive control, enabling the operator to set and adjust impeller rotational speed from 2,000 RPM to 12,000 RPM in discrete increments. The software translates user input from the touchscreen interface into motor drive commands via a closed-loop speed control algorithm.
>
> • **Aspiration Pump Control** --- The software regulates the aspiration pump to achieve and maintain the operator-selected negative pressure level, from −200 mmHg to −650 mmHg. Pressure feedback from an inline sensor is used in a closed-loop control algorithm to maintain target vacuum.
>
> • **User Interface** --- The console features a 7-inch color touchscreen display managed by the embedded software. The display presents real-time impeller speed, aspiration pressure, operational mode status, procedure elapsed time, and alarm/alert notifications.
>
> • **Safety Monitoring** --- The software continuously monitors key parameters and activates safety alarms and automatic shutdowns in response to fault conditions, including over-speed detection (impeller exceeding 12,500 RPM), over-pressure alarm (aspiration exceeding −700 mmHg), and catheter connection status (detection of catheter disconnect during operation).
>
> • **Operational Data Logging** --- The software records operational parameters (speed, pressure, alarms, error codes) to an internal non-volatile memory log for post-procedure review and service diagnostics.

The console software is developed and maintained by Clearfield\'s in-house software engineering team. The software operates on a proprietary embedded platform utilizing a dedicated microcontroller; no off-the-shelf operating system (OS) is employed. The software is written in C and compiled for the target hardware platform using a validated toolchain.

**[5.2 --- Software Level of Concern]{.underline}**

Based on assessment per FDA\'s *Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices* (2005), the console software is classified as **Minor** level of concern.

The rationale for this classification is as follows: the software provides user interface and motor control functions for the VascuClear Console. The console includes independent hardware safety mechanisms that operate outside of, and independently from, the software control system. Specifically, the console incorporates a mechanical over-speed governor that physically limits impeller speed regardless of software commands, and a hardware pressure relief valve that prevents aspiration pressure from exceeding a safe threshold independent of software control. These redundant hardware safety mechanisms mitigate risks associated with software malfunction. Accordingly, a software failure would not be expected to result in death or serious injury, because the hardware safety systems would independently prevent the most critical hazard scenarios.

Under the 2005 FDA guidance framework, \"Major\" level of concern is applicable when a software failure could directly result in death or serious injury to the patient or operator, or when software is the sole mitigation for a hazardous condition. \"Moderate\" level of concern applies when a software failure could result in minor injury or when software failure is unlikely to result in serious injury given the overall system design. \"Minor\" level of concern applies when a software failure would not be expected to cause injury, or would result in only inconvenience or transient discomfort.

Given the independent hardware safety mechanisms in the VascuClear Console, the residual risk from software failure is assessed as insufficient to warrant a Moderate or Major classification. The Minor classification is therefore considered appropriate for the console firmware.

**[5.3 --- Proposed Software V&V Activities]{.underline}**

Consistent with the Minor level of concern classification, the following software documentation and testing activities are proposed for inclusion in the 510(k) submission:

1\. **Software Requirements Specification (SRS)** --- A complete document defining all functional, performance, interface, and safety requirements for the console software. The SRS will serve as the basis for all subsequent verification and validation activities.

2\. **Software Design Specification (SDS)** --- A description of the software architecture and design, including module structure, data flow, and control logic. For Minor level of concern, the SDS will be provided at a summary level sufficient to demonstrate the relationship between requirements and design elements.

3\. **Traceability Matrix** --- A bidirectional traceability matrix linking each software requirement (SRS) to corresponding design elements (SDS) and to specific test cases. This matrix will demonstrate complete traceability from requirements through design to verification.

4\. **Unit Testing** --- Code-level testing of individual software modules performed by the development team. A code coverage target of greater than 80% (statement coverage) is established. Unit test results and code coverage reports will be summarized in the submission.

5\. **Integration Testing** --- Testing of software modules in combination to verify correct inter-module communication, data exchange, and control flow. Integration test protocols and results will be documented.

6\. **System-Level Testing** --- End-to-end testing of the console software against the SRS, performed on production-equivalent console hardware. System-level testing will be conducted by Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc. as an independent verification and validation activity. Test cases will cover all SRS requirements including normal operation, boundary conditions, and selected fault scenarios.

Consistent with the Minor level of concern classification, hazard analysis documentation, detailed software architecture documentation, and unresolved software anomaly lists are available for internal review but are not proposed for inclusion in the 510(k) submission. These documents will be provided upon specific request from CDRH during the review process.

Software V&V activities will be performed in accordance with Clearfield\'s internal software development lifecycle procedures. Independent system-level testing will be conducted at Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc.

**[Section 6 --- Sterilization Validation]{.underline}**

**[6.1 --- Sterilization Method]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Catheter is provided to the end user as a sterile, single-use device. Each catheter is packaged in a peelable pouch package (Tyvek/polyester film laminate construction) sealed using a validated heat-seal process. The catheter is terminally sterilized within its sealed package.

The sterilization method employed is ethylene oxide (EtO) sterilization, validated in accordance with ISO 11135:2014, *Sterilization of health-care products --- Ethylene oxide --- Requirements for the development, validation and routine control of a sterilization process for medical devices*. Clearfield has selected EtO sterilization based on the thermal sensitivity of the polyurethane/Pebax catheter materials, which are not compatible with steam or dry heat sterilization, and the complex device geometry that may limit radiation sterilization dose uniformity.

Sterilization validation studies will be performed at Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories, a GLP-compliant, ISO 17025-accredited contract testing facility. The validation program will include the following elements:

> • **Half-Cycle Qualification** --- Demonstration that the minimum sterilization cycle (half the routine exposure time) achieves the required sterility assurance level.
>
> • **Bioburden Determination** --- Characterization of the pre-sterilization microbial load on production catheter units per ISO 11737-1.
>
> • **Sterility Testing** --- Verification of sterility of product samples processed through the sterilization cycle per ISO 11737-2.
>
> • **EtO Residual Analysis** --- Quantification of ethylene oxide (EtO) and ethylene chlorohydrin (ECH) residuals on sterilized catheters per ISO 10993-7 to confirm residual levels are within acceptable patient exposure limits.

The target sterility assurance level (SAL) is 10⁻⁶, consistent with FDA expectations and industry practice for terminally sterilized medical devices.

The VascuClear Console is a reusable device and is not subject to sterilization validation. Cleaning and disinfection instructions for the console will be provided in the Instructions for Use (IFU) and validated separately under Clearfield\'s reusable device reprocessing validation protocol.

**[6.2 --- Sterilization Validation Protocol Summary]{.underline}**

The sterilization validation protocol will define the EtO sterilization process parameters, including EtO gas concentration (450--700 mg/L), chamber temperature (45--55°C), relative humidity (50--80%), and exposure time. The routine sterilization cycle parameters will be established based on process development studies, with the half-cycle qualification serving as the primary validation approach.

**Half-Cycle Validation Approach.** The half-cycle method will be used to demonstrate a sterility assurance level (SAL) of 10⁻⁶. Under this approach, product samples will be exposed to EtO at half the routine cycle exposure time. If the sterility test results demonstrate no growth in all tested samples, the routine full-cycle process (2× the half-cycle exposure time) is considered validated to achieve a SAL of 10⁻⁶. This approach is consistent with ISO 11135:2014 and the AAMI TIR16 methodology.

**Bioburden Characterization.** Pre-sterilization bioburden will be determined on a minimum of 30 catheter units sampled from three separate production lots (10 units per lot). Bioburden testing will be performed per ISO 11737-1 using membrane filtration and pour plate culture methods. Bioburden data will be used to support the sterilization cycle design and to establish routine bioburden monitoring limits.

**EtO Residual Analysis.** Ethylene oxide and ethylene chlorohydrin residual levels will be measured on sterilized catheter samples at multiple time points following sterilization (0, 24, 48, and 72 hours post-aeration, and at the end of the routine aeration period). Residuals will be quantified using gas chromatography (GC) methods per ISO 10993-7. Acceptance criteria are based on the ISO 10993-7 limits for contact devices with limited exposure duration: ethylene oxide ≤ 4 mg per device and ethylene chlorohydrin ≤ 9 mg per device.

**Sample Sizes and Acceptance Criteria.** A minimum of 20 catheter units per half-cycle qualification run will be tested for sterility (3 qualification runs, for a total of 60 units). Acceptance criterion: no growth in 100% of test samples. EtO residual testing: minimum of 10 samples per time point. Acceptance criterion: all samples below ISO 10993-7 limits.

Validation protocols will be prepared under Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories protocol numbers (to be assigned upon study initiation). Protocol numbers and final reports will be referenced in the 510(k) submission.

**[Section 7 --- Simulated-Use Bench Performance Testing]{.underline}**

**[7.1 --- Test Setup]{.underline}**

Simulated-use bench performance testing is designed to evaluate the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System\'s thrombus removal capability under conditions that approximate clinical use in a controlled laboratory environment. The primary objective is to demonstrate that the device can effectively remove thrombus from a simulated iliofemoral venous segment.

The bench test model consists of a silicone vascular anatomy model that replicates the iliofemoral venous segment. The model specifications are as follows:

> • **Inner Diameter:** The model replicates three venous segments: common iliac vein (inner diameter 12--15 mm), external iliac vein (inner diameter 10--13 mm), and common femoral vein (inner diameter 10--12 mm).
>
> • **Vessel Length:** The total model length is approximately 30 cm, encompassing the three venous segments in anatomically correct sequence.
>
> • **Physiologic Curvature:** The model incorporates physiologic curvature and angulation representative of the pelvic venous anatomy, including the junction of the common iliac veins and the inguinal ligament transition zone.
>
> • **Circulating Fluid:** Normal saline at 37°C, circulated through the model at a flow rate of 200--400 mL/min, simulating resting venous hemodynamic conditions in the iliofemoral segment.
>
> • **Thrombus Analog:** Synthetic thrombus material consisting of bovine blood clot (or, alternatively, a synthetic polymer-based clot analog of equivalent mechanical properties) prepared to a standardized weight of 2.0 ± 0.2 g. Thrombus analogs will be aged 24--48 hours at 37°C prior to testing. Each thrombus analog is positioned within the external iliac vein segment of the model prior to each test run.

All simulated-use bench testing will be conducted at Clearfield Medical Devices\' internal R&D laboratory in Minnetonka, Minnesota.

**[7.2 --- Test Protocol and Endpoints]{.underline}**

The simulated-use bench test procedure is as follows: The VascuClear Catheter is introduced over a standard 0.035-inch guidewire into the silicone vascular model via the common femoral vein segment. The catheter is advanced under fluoroscopic or direct visual guidance until the distal impeller tip is positioned at the proximal edge of the thrombus analog. The VascuClear Console is then activated at operator-selected settings within the clinically intended range: impeller speed of 8,000--12,000 RPM and aspiration pressure of −400 to −650 mmHg. A single pass thrombectomy is performed, advancing the catheter through the full length of the thrombus analog and withdrawing. Following the single pass, the catheter is removed from the model.

**Primary Endpoint.** The primary endpoint is the percentage of thrombus removed by weight after a single pass thrombectomy. Thrombus removal percentage is calculated as:

> Thrombus Removal (%) = \[(Pre-procedure thrombus weight − Post-procedure residual thrombus weight) / Pre-procedure thrombus weight\] × 100

The acceptance criterion for the primary endpoint is ≥80% thrombus removal by weight after a single pass.

**Secondary Endpoints.** The secondary endpoints include: (a) procedural time to complete the single-pass thrombectomy (from console activation to catheter withdrawal), measured in seconds; (b) volume of aspirated material collected in the console aspiration canister, measured in mL; and (c) vessel wall integrity, assessed by visual inspection of the silicone model post-test for evidence of perforation, dissection, or significant surface abrasion.

**Sample Size and Pass/Fail Criteria.** A minimum of twenty (n = 20) test runs will be performed across a minimum of five unique catheter units (with each catheter unit used for up to four test runs, provided it remains structurally intact). The overall acceptance criteria are:

> • ≥80% thrombus removal by weight achieved in ≥90% of test runs (i.e., a minimum of 18 out of 20 test runs);
>
> • No vessel wall perforation in any test run;
>
> • No catheter component failure (fracture, detachment, or malfunction) in any test run.

Of note, the simulated-use bench testing protocol evaluates mechanical thrombectomy only. The thrombolytic infusion lumen is not activated during simulated-use bench testing. Combined pharmacomechanical testing (simultaneous mechanical thrombectomy with concurrent thrombolytic agent delivery) is not included in this bench test protocol. The drug delivery characterization testing described in Section 8 is performed separately under different test conditions.

**[Section 8 --- Drug Delivery Characterization Testing]{.underline}**

**[8.1 --- Overview]{.underline}**

The VascuClear Catheter includes a proprietary thrombolytic infusion lumen designed for simultaneous local delivery of thrombolytic agents at the thrombectomy site. This integrated infusion lumen is a distinguishing feature of the VascuClear 3000 system and is central to the device\'s intended use for pharmacomechanical thrombectomy. The infusion lumen allows the treating physician to deliver physician-specified thrombolytic agents --- including tissue plasminogen activator (tPA/alteplase) --- directly to the site of thrombus during or after the mechanical thrombectomy procedure.

The purpose of the drug delivery characterization testing described in this section is to verify the flow performance of the infusion lumen and confirm that it delivers fluid at the specified flow rates accurately and reliably. This testing is intended to support the drug delivery functionality described in the proposed indications for use and the device labeling.

**[8.2 --- Proposed Test Protocol]{.underline}**

The drug delivery characterization test will be performed as follows:

**Test Fluid.** Normal saline (0.9% NaCl solution) at room temperature (20--25°C) will be used as the test fluid for all drug delivery characterization testing.

**Flow Rate Verification.** The proximal end of the VascuClear Catheter infusion lumen will be connected to a calibrated syringe pump (Harvard Apparatus or equivalent). Saline will be delivered through the infusion lumen at five discrete flow rate settings: 2 mL/min, 4 mL/min, 6 mL/min, 8 mL/min, and 10 mL/min. At each flow rate setting, the syringe pump will operate for a 10-minute delivery period. The volume of saline exiting the distal end of the infusion lumen will be collected in a calibrated graduated cylinder over the 10-minute period, and the actual delivered flow rate will be calculated by dividing the collected volume by the delivery time. The target for delivered flow rate accuracy is ±10% of the set flow rate at each of the five tested settings.

**Infusion Pressure Measurement.** An inline pressure transducer (calibrated and traceable to NIST standards) will be positioned between the syringe pump and the proximal end of the infusion lumen. Infusion pressure will be continuously recorded during each flow rate test. At each flow rate setting, the maximum and steady-state infusion pressures will be documented. The acceptance criterion is that the measured infusion pressure shall remain below the catheter\'s rated infusion lumen burst pressure at all tested flow rates.

**Lumen Patency Test.** Following completion of the flow rate verification tests, the infusion lumen will be subjected to a sustained patency test. Saline will be delivered continuously at the maximum flow rate (10 mL/min) for a period of 60 minutes. At the conclusion of the 60-minute infusion, the lumen will be inspected for evidence of occlusion, blockage, or reduction in flow. The infusion lumen will also be visually inspected (under magnification) for leakage at any point along its length, at the distal tip, and at the proximal hub connection. The acceptance criterion is that the infusion lumen remains fully patent with no detectable occlusion, and no leakage is observed at any point.

**Sample Size.** A minimum of ten (n = 10) VascuClear Catheter units will be tested across the complete drug delivery characterization protocol (flow rate verification at all five settings, infusion pressure measurement, and lumen patency test).

**Acceptance Criteria Summary.** The acceptance criteria for the drug delivery characterization testing are:

> • Delivered flow rate at each of the five tested settings is within ±10% of the set flow rate;
>
> • Infusion pressure at all tested flow rates remains below the rated burst pressure of the infusion lumen;
>
> • No occlusion, blockage, or reduction in flow after 60 minutes of continuous saline infusion at maximum flow rate;
>
> • No leakage observed at any point along the infusion lumen, distal tip, or proximal hub connection.

The above testing is considered sufficient to characterize the drug delivery functionality of the VascuClear infusion lumen for purposes of the 510(k) submission.

**[Section 9 --- Summary and Testing Timeline]{.underline}**

**[9.1 --- Summary of Proposed Testing]{.underline}**

The following table provides a comprehensive summary of all proposed testing for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System in support of the traditional 510(k) submission:

**Table 9-1: VascuClear 3000 Proposed Testing Summary**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Test Category**                                                                                                      **Applicable Standard / Method**                                    **Testing Facility**                 **Est. Duration**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------ -------------------
  Biocompatibility (cytotoxicity, sensitization, irritation, acute systemic toxicity, hemocompatibility, pyrogenicity)   ISO 10993-1:2018 series (ISO 10993-3, -4, -5, -10, -11, -18, -23)   Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories   12--16 weeks

  Mechanical Performance (pushability, torqueability, kink resistance, tensile strength, burst pressure)                 Internal test methods; ASTM references                              Clearfield R&D Lab, Minnetonka, MN   4--6 weeks

  Impeller Performance and Fatigue (500 rotational cycles)                                                               Internal test method                                                Clearfield R&D Lab, Minnetonka, MN   2--3 weeks

  Electrical Safety (IEC 60601-1)                                                                                        IEC 60601-1:2012                                                    Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc.     8--10 weeks

  Software Verification and Validation (Minor level of concern)                                                          FDA Guidance (2005) for Software in Medical Devices                 Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc.     6--8 weeks

  Sterilization Validation (EtO)                                                                                         ISO 11135:2014; ISO 11737-1; ISO 11737-2; ISO 10993-7               Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories   12--16 weeks

  Simulated-Use Bench Performance Testing                                                                                Internal test method                                                Clearfield R&D Lab, Minnetonka, MN   4--6 weeks

  Drug Delivery Characterization (saline flow testing)                                                                   Internal test method                                                Clearfield R&D Lab, Minnetonka, MN   2--3 weeks
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The total estimated testing timeline, assuming parallel execution of independent testing tracks, is approximately 16--20 weeks from initiation of the first study. Biocompatibility and sterilization validation studies represent the critical path at 12--16 weeks each. Mechanical, bench performance, and drug delivery characterization studies will be conducted in parallel at Clearfield\'s internal R&D laboratory. Electrical safety and software V&V testing will proceed concurrently at Pinnacle Standards Testing.

The total estimated testing cost for the program described above is approximately \$600,000, inclusive of all contract laboratory fees, internal testing costs, sample fabrication, and report preparation. This budget is allocated within the overall 510(k) preparation and submission budget of \$1.4 million.

Clearfield Medical Devices believes that the testing program described in this document, when executed and combined with the device description, predicate comparison, and clinical data outlined in the companion Pre-Submission documents, will provide an adequate evidentiary foundation for a substantial equivalence determination by CDRH.

**[9.2 --- Proposed Testing Timeline]{.underline}**

The proposed timeline for execution of the VascuClear 3000 testing program is as follows:

**Target Testing Initiation:** July 2025, following receipt of FDA feedback from the anticipated Q-Sub meeting (scheduled for August/September 2025). Clearfield intends to finalize test protocols based on FDA feedback and initiate the first studies promptly thereafter. Where FDA feedback does not materially alter proposed protocols, certain preparatory activities (sample fabrication, protocol finalization, laboratory scheduling) may commence prior to the Q-Sub meeting.

**Parallel Testing Tracks:**

> • **Track 1 (Critical Path):** Biocompatibility studies and sterilization validation --- longest lead time at 12--16 weeks. These studies will be initiated first at Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories.
>
> • **Track 2:** Electrical safety testing at Pinnacle Standards Testing --- estimated 8--10 weeks. Can proceed in parallel with Track 1 upon availability of production-equivalent console units.
>
> • **Track 3:** Mechanical performance testing, simulated-use bench testing, and drug delivery characterization at Clearfield R&D Lab --- estimated 4--6 weeks total. These studies will be initiated concurrently with Tracks 1 and 2.
>
> • **Track 4:** Software verification and validation --- estimated 6--8 weeks. Documentation activities (SRS, SDS, traceability matrix) can begin immediately; independent system-level testing at Pinnacle will follow completion of documentation.

**Anticipated Completion of All Testing:** November through December 2025, based on a July 2025 initiation date and the 16--20 week overall timeline.

**510(k) Submission Target:** Clearfield\'s goal is to submit the complete 510(k) premarket notification as early as practical following completion of all testing activities, consistent with the company\'s milestone target of achieving 510(k) clearance by December 31, 2025. Ridgepoint Consulting Group will coordinate testing activities with all contract laboratories and manage integration of testing deliverables into the 510(k) submission documentation package.

**[Section 10 --- Questions for FDA Feedback]{.underline}**

Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. respectfully requests CDRH feedback on the following specific questions regarding the proposed testing plan for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System:

**Question 1 --- Biocompatibility Testing Endpoints.** Does FDA concur with the proposed biocompatibility testing endpoints for the VascuClear Catheter based on the device contact duration classification? Specifically, does FDA agree that the endpoints listed in Table 2-1 of this document are appropriate and sufficient for a blood-contacting catheter with limited contact duration, and that the exclusion of subchronic implantation, subchronic systemic toxicity, and genotoxicity testing is justified on the basis of the limited contact duration classification?

**Question 2 --- Mechanical Performance and Impeller Fatigue Testing.** Does FDA concur that the proposed mechanical performance testing program, including the catheter mechanical properties tests described in Section 3.2 and the impeller fatigue protocol described in Section 3.3, is adequate to support a substantial equivalence determination for the VascuClear Catheter? Does FDA have any recommendations regarding additional mechanical testing or modifications to the proposed fatigue test protocol?

**Question 3 --- Electrical Safety Testing.** Does FDA concur that the proposed electrical safety testing program per IEC 60601-1:2012, as described in Section 4, is sufficient for the VascuClear Console? Are there additional electrical safety concerns specific to the console\'s electromechanical drive and aspiration pump functionality that should be addressed?

**Question 4 --- Software Level of Concern and Documentation.** Is the proposed software documentation package --- consistent with a Minor level of concern classification --- appropriate for the VascuClear Console firmware? Does FDA agree with the Minor level of concern determination based on the rationale provided in Section 5.2, considering the presence of independent hardware safety mechanisms?

**Question 5 --- Simulated-Use Bench Testing.** Does FDA concur that the proposed bench testing protocol utilizing synthetic thrombus analog material in a silicone vascular anatomy model is an acceptable method for demonstrating thrombus removal efficacy? Does FDA have recommendations regarding the thrombus analog material, sample size, or acceptance criteria?

**Question 6 --- Drug Delivery Characterization.** Is the proposed drug delivery characterization testing with normal saline, as described in Section 8.2, sufficient to characterize the infusion lumen\'s flow performance for purposes of the 510(k) submission? Or does FDA recommend additional testing with thrombolytic agents (e.g., tPA/alteplase) or other fluids to more completely characterize the drug delivery functionality?

Clearfield appreciates CDRH\'s consideration of these questions and looks forward to a productive Pre-Submission feedback interaction. Clearfield is prepared to modify the proposed testing plan based on FDA\'s recommendations.

**[Appendix A --- Device Specifications Summary Table]{.underline}**

The following table provides a side-by-side comparison of key technical specifications for the VascuClear 3000 Thrombectomy System (subject device) and the identified predicate device, the ThrombEx 200 (K192847).

**Table A-1: Device Specification Comparison**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  ---------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------

  510(k) Number                      Pending                                                        K192847

  Clearance Date                     Pending                                                        August 14, 2020

  Product Code                       GXD                                                            GXD

  Regulation                         21 CFR 870.1250                                                21 CFR 870.1250

  Catheter Diameter                  10 Fr                                                          8 Fr

  Working Length                     65 cm                                                          55 cm

  Thrombectomy Mechanism             Rotating helical impeller                                      Bernoulli-effect aspiration

  Maximum Impeller Speed             12,000 RPM                                                     N/A (no rotating element)

  Aspiration Pressure                −650 mmHg (console-integrated pump)                            Not specified in 510(k) summary

  Drug Delivery Lumen                Yes --- integrated thrombolytic infusion lumen                 No

  Console / Drive Unit               VascuClear Console (reusable, AC-powered)                      Vasogen Controller (reusable)

  Sterilization (Catheter)           EtO                                                            EtO

  Single Use / Reusable (Catheter)   Single use                                                     Single use

  Intended Use Population            Adults with acute DVT (≤14 days), iliofemoral venous segment   Adults with acute DVT, iliofemoral venous segment
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This table is provided for reference purposes and should be read in conjunction with the detailed predicate comparison document included in the Pre-Submission package.

**[Appendix B --- Testing Laboratory Information]{.underline}**

**[Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories]{.underline}**

Whitecrest Analytical Laboratories is a contract research and testing organization specializing in biocompatibility evaluation and sterilization validation for medical devices. Whitecrest\'s facilities are located in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina. The laboratory operates in compliance with FDA Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations (21 CFR Part 58) and holds ISO 17025 accreditation from the American Association for Laboratory Accreditation (A2LA). Whitecrest will perform all biocompatibility testing (ISO 10993 series) and sterilization validation (ISO 11135, ISO 11737, ISO 10993-7) studies for the VascuClear 3000 program.

**[Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc.]{.underline}**

Pinnacle Standards Testing, Inc. is an independent testing laboratory specializing in electrical safety evaluation and software verification and validation for medical electrical equipment. Pinnacle is an OSHA Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory (NRTL) and holds accreditation for testing to IEC 60601-1 and related IEC 60601 series standards. Pinnacle\'s facilities are located in Austin, Texas. Pinnacle will perform all IEC 60601-1 electrical safety testing and independent system-level software verification and validation for the VascuClear Console.

**[Clearfield Medical Devices, Inc. --- Internal R&D Laboratory]{.underline}**

Clearfield Medical Devices\' internal R&D testing laboratory is located at the company\'s headquarters facility in Minnetonka, Minnesota. The laboratory is equipped for mechanical performance testing, simulated-use bench testing, and drug delivery characterization studies. Internal testing is conducted under Clearfield\'s Quality Management System (ISO 13485:2016 certified) with calibrated, traceable instrumentation. Clearfield\'s R&D laboratory will perform all mechanical performance testing (Section 3), simulated-use bench testing (Section 7), and drug delivery characterization testing (Section 8) for the VascuClear 3000 program.

**--- End of Document ---**
